1277626.dis_text: We still celebrate Thanksgiving today with a traditional dinner. The kitchen is always the most crowded room in our house because we all help prepare the food. We often talk a lot and tell stories after dinner as well. When it is all over, everyone helps wash the dishes. The festival is a very busy time for travel when friends and families come together to celebrate.

title.text:
W: Frank, tomorrow is Mother's Day.
M: What should we buy for our mom as a gift?
W: What about flowers?
M: I don't think they're a good gift.
W: How about a watch?
M: Pretty good! Let's go shopping this afternoon. And I think we should take Mom out for a big dinner tomorrow.
W: OK. There is a new Chinese restaurant on Green Street. We can try some Chinese food there.
M: That's a good idea.

M: Can I help you?
W: Yes, please. I'm looking for a Father's Day gift.
M: Well, what about a wallet?
W: Sounds good! How much is the black wallet?
M: Oh, it's $68.
W: It's kind of expensive. Do you have a cheaper one?
M: How about this yellow one? It's $28.
W: I don't think my father will like it.
M: How about this tie?
W: Oh, it looks good. I think my father will like it very much.

W: Hello, Eric. This is Nancy. What are you doing?
M: Hello, Nancy. I'm watching a football match on TV. What's up?
W: Tom is going to have a party next Tuesday afternoon. Would you like to go with me?
M: I'd love to, but I'm afraid I can't.
W: Why not?
M: Because I have to fly back to America for Thanksgiving Day.
W: Oh, what a pity! I thought you could come!
M: Never mind. I'll have a party when I come back to Beijing. My cousin Jack will come back with me.
W: Really? Please introduce me to Jack! I have heard so much about him from you.
M: OK!

1277628.title_text: My name is Lisa. Last Sunday was the National Day. My relatives and I did different things to celebrate it. My grandparents went to Zhongshan Park. They walked around the park and enjoyed the flowers there. My parents and my brother went to Tian'anmen Square in the morning. They saw the flag-raising ceremony. My aunt went shopping. There were many things on sale in the shopping mall. My cousin didn't go to the shopping mall with my aunt. Instead, he went to a bookshop near his home. As for me, I climbed a mountain with my friends because the weather was great.

model_info.dis_text: We still celebrate Thanksgiving today with a traditional dinner. The kitchen is always the most crowded room in our house because we all help prepare the food. We lay the table, and then before we begin dinner, my father gives thanks for the food, so we remember why we celebrate the festival. We usually eat too much, but it is only once a year! We often talk a lot and tell stories after dinner as well. When it is all over, everyone helps wash the dishes. The festival is a very busy time for travel when friends and families come together to celebrate.
